LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER RELIABLE TREE EXPERTS NOTICE OF HEARING DATE FOR RELIABLE TREE EXPERTS' WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS CONTINUED FROM JAN-19-2010 TO LAW AND MOTION AT MAR-18-2010, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CONTINUED TO MARCH 18, 2010, PER STIPULATION. JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER RELIABLE TREE EXPERTS NOTICE OF HEARING DATE FOR RELIABLE TREE EXPERTS' WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: WRIT IS DENIED. THE COURT EMPLOYS INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT/DE NOVO REVIEW AND ACCORDS LITTLE TO NO DEFERENCE TO RESPONDENT'S DECISION. PETITIONER WAS HIRED TO PRUNE AND REMOVE TREES, AND APPROXIMATELY 80% OF THE WORK INVOLVED REMOVAL. REMOVING AND PRUNING TREES IS "USUAL" WORK WITH REGARD TO ROADWAYS, AND PETITIONER'S WORK MEETS THE DEFINITION OF MAINTENANCE UNDER 8 CCR 16000. COURTS WILL LIBERALLY CONSTRUE PREVAILING WAGE STATUTES. (SEE STATE BUILDING & CONSTRUCTION TRADES COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA VS. DUNCAN (2008) 162 CAL.APP. 4TH 289, 306.) THE SAN BERNARDINO DECISION IS NOT BINDING, THAT TREE REMOVAL WAS PERFORMED ON PRIVATELY OWNED RESIDENTIAL LAND, AND WAS A ONE-TIME PROJECT DESIGNED TO REMOVE DEAD, DYING, OR DISEASED TREES. HERE, THE PROJECT INVOLVES A NORMAL AND RECURRING ACTIVITY OF MAINTAINING THE PUBLIC HIGHWAY. (THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD IS RETURNED TO MR. CUMMING, AT THE HEARING.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing Date For Reliable Tree Experts' Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Mar-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER RELIABLE TREE EXPERTS Notice of Hearing Date for Reliable Tree Experts' Writ of Administrative Mandamus. Appearances: Jason Curliano, Esq. Buty & Curliano LLP (510) 267-3000 appearing for Petitioner and Moving party; Alice L. Ramsey, & Cheryl Pirtle, Deputy Attorneys, Dept. of Transportation. (916) 654-2630, & John Cumming, Dept. of Industrial Relations, State of California (415) 703-4240, appearing for respondent and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Writ is denied. The Court employs independent judgment/de novo review and accords little to no deference to Respondent's decision. Petitioner was hired to prune and remove trees, and approximately 80% of the work involved removal. Removing and pruning trees is "usual" work with regard to roadways, and petitioner's work meets the definition of maintenance under 8 CCR 16000. Courts will liberally construe prevailing wage statutes. (See State Building & Construction Trades Council of California vs. Duncan (2008) 162 Cal.App. 4th 289, 306.) The San Bernardino decision is not binding, that tree removal was performed on privately owned residential land, and was a one-time project designed to remove dead, dying, or diseased trees. Here, the project involves a normal and recurring activity of maintaining the public highway. (The Administrative record is returned to Mr. Cumming, at the hearing.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.)

Notice Of Hearing Date For Reliable Tree Experts' Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus
